mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment rate crossing the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer demand and residential investment, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60d returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output and supply-side contraction, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during periods of rising inflation.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines produce positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between the federal funds rate and the annualized CPI inflation rate indicates real interest rate compression, which boosts equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The real rate differential shows no predictive power for 20d returns, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio below 0.5 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue potential, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with positive 60d returns, yielding a Rank IC below 0.03 across all rolling 2-year test periods.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real gross domestic product signals an economic recession that immediately triggers risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5d returns in > 55% of quarters where GDPC1 contracts, invalidating the recession risk-off signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production coinciding with stable or falling unemployment indicates healthy capacity utilization without labor market overheating, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during mid-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: The combination of rising INDPRO and stable UNRATE yields negative 20d returns in > 60% of triggered cases during mid-cycle periods.
